reviews.author: Major
reviews.text: There are three hills with small park between them. From hills there are nice view of the city and mountain in a distance. Also there is like remains of old fortification walls. The hills are not high much so you can climb them with ease and the nature is beautiful. Is was a little bit difficult to find right entrance to place but after a moment and one detour I've founded it :)

reviews.author: PK
reviews.text: A nice view close from the city. There's a few places where you can go up the hills from, and the climb up isn't that difficult. When raining it can get a bit slippery. Best visited when it's sunny outside, but it was still nice when it was cloudy when I went.

reviews.author: Tran xuan Thuy
reviews.text: Not so much left but good sights seeing place

reviews.author: Maria O
reviews.text: This place was an awesome surprise. We went in March and the day was clear enough that we could see many of the mountains surrounding Lang Son. It's free, and we walked here, it took about 15 min from our accommodation. We got here before a bus load of tourists so we had the entire place to ourselves. There are 5 peaks to hike up (one of them is Nang Tu Thi, with the stone woman) all fairly easy. Some have stairs and some have trails you can easily follow. The place is fairly clean compared to many other places I have seen in Vietnam. But do not go too far into the bush as we did see needles in the bush when we recovered my drone. If you're an off the beaten path traveler, this place is for you. You can see the other 2 nearby caves as well, all within a 10 min walk of eachother. You can do this independently, no tours and no one will bother you here. One of the caves is around the corner, and the street leading up to it has all kinds of food stalls for drinks, food, toilets and souvenirs if you're into that. But they aren't aggressive and won't bother you like they do in highly touristic areas. Enjoy this spot!! I had no idea what it was before I came and was well worth it, we spent 2-3 hours total here exploring each peak.

reviews.author: Đương Nguyễn
reviews.text: The air is good, the food is delicious and the people are so friendly.

reviews.author: Vu Hung Nguyen (Hưng)
reviews.text: A good castle in term of defense, it is in a high place so the soldiers can watch the whole city. However, it is too small. Not so impressive.

reviews.author: Antoni Mysliborski
reviews.text: Worth visiting if you're around - not much too see of the ruins, but you can climb some of the karst hills to see the view of the city and surrounding mountains

reviews.author: Linh Duy
reviews.text: Nice place to have a great view of the city

reviews.author: Barbora Tomanová
reviews.text: The ruins aren't that interesting, but the view from the tops are astonishing. A lot of stairs, but really worth seeing.

reviews.author: Pham Tuyet Mai
reviews.text: Not much relics left to see but it remains interesting to visit such a historical place in Lang Son.

reviews.author: Khoi Pham Viet Ngoc
reviews.text: Nice historic place to learn about vietnam history

reviews.author: Văn Lam Lưu
reviews.text: Scenic spot when you get to Lang Sơn

reviews.author: Josef Kulhánek
reviews.text: Great view over the whole city.
